marketState: neutral — extreme 8-16d heat catalyst building against near-term demand softness and elevated storage
summary: Front NG=F ($3.151) sits $0.19 above the $2.958 fair-value midpoint but remains well inside the $2.46–$3.46 band, signaling modest overvaluation rather than an outright short. The near-term fundamental picture is bearish: 1–7 day demand is running 13% below the 5-year average and the latest storage report printed a bearish +25 Bcf surprise on top of a pre-existing +153 Bcf surplus. However, current demand has already surged +21 GWd in 24 hours to 158.3 — 25% above the 5-year normal and at the top of the 7-day range — while the 8–16 day forecast is extreme at +54% above the 5-year average and above the historical maximum, signalling a powerful heat wave is en route. Managed-money net positioning is at the 17th 5-year percentile (−84,909 net short), a historically extreme short base that creates significant squeeze potential as the heat event enters the market's pricing window within 2–5 trading days. Three-week seasonality supports longs at 69% probability, daily and weekly MACD are both positive, and the winter strip (Dec-26 $3.97 / Jan-27 $4.37) prices a $1.22 seasonal premium, providing structural long-term support.

longTerm.rationale: Three-week seasonality is 69% bullish and managed-money net short sits at the 17th 5-year percentile — crowded short positioning creates a powerful squeeze catalyst should the extreme 8–16 day heat demand (above historical max) sustain into the injection season. Fair value migrates to FAIR status by day 21 (~$3.065 midpoint, $2.565–$3.565 band), reducing mean-reversion headwinds over the horizon; the upper band edge near $3.46–$3.57 offers roughly 2.7:1 R:R from a $3.10 entry with a $2.97 stop. Storage surplus (+153 Bcf) is the primary structural bear case and caps conviction at medium.
longTerm.invalidation: A weekly close below $2.97 (fair-value midpoint) signals the market is repricing lower; a second consecutive injection of +70 Bcf+ widening the surplus beyond +200 Bcf vs 5-year would also invalidate.

shortTerm.rationale: Short-timeframe RSI is near oversold (30–34 on 5m/15m/1h) with today's demand already at the top of the 7-day range (+21 GWd in 24h), pointing toward limited near-term downside. Within 2–5 trading days the extreme 8–16 day heat forecast will fully enter the market's pricing window, forcing net-short managed money (17th 5-year percentile) to cover — a dynamic that compresses moves quickly in NG. Sizing is reduced to 0.75R to account for the 1–7 day demand weakness (−13% vs 5y) and the bearish storage surprise as early-week headwinds that could test the stop zone before the heat catalyst lifts prices.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sustained close below $3.00 on volume, or if 8–16 day heat models trend materially cooler in the next 24–48 hours of model updates, removes the weather-squeeze thesis.

keyDrivers: Extreme 8–16 day heat demand: +54% above the 5-year average and above the historical maximum — the dominant near-term bullish catalyst
Managed-money net short at 17th 5-year percentile (−84,909 contracts): extreme crowded positioning creates squeeze risk on sustained heat confirmation
Storage surplus +153 Bcf vs 5-year average with a bearish +25 Bcf injection surprise: primary structural headwind capping upside and limiting conviction
3-week seasonality 69% bullish; 4-week only 31% with LOW reliability (44 pp 10–90% spread) — timing uncertainty elevated
Winter strip (Dec-26 $3.97 / Jan-27 $4.37) prices $1.22 seasonal premium vs front, embedding a bullish long-dated market consensus
risks: Storage surplus widens further if heat disappoints or LNG export volumes slip, pressuring price toward or below the $2.97 fair-value midpoint
8–16 day heat forecast moderates in model updates over the next 24–48 hours, removing the primary catalyst and triggering additional managed-money short selling
Seasonal reliability is LOW with a 44 pp 10–90% spread; the 4-week pattern is only 31% bullish, raising the risk of a mid-summer roll-off even if early weeks are firm
